meta.title: Men's / Hero-Product Fragrance Website Design Audit
meta.methodology: Expert visual + UX review grounded in real full-page screenshots (captured via thum.io) and product-page content analysis (via web fetch). Perceived speed only — no Lighthouse runs. Each criterion scored 1–10, then weighted to a /100 score.

sites.name: Snif
sites.notes: Witty, gender-inclusive voice. Notes shown 3 ways (inline list, Top/Mid/Base pyramid, expandable ingredient cards) plus a 'scent stack' visual. 7-day try-at-home, subscribe & save, bundles. Accessible price ($68/30ml) lowers risk. Best conversion architecture in the set.

sites.name: Commodity
sites.notes: Dramatic B&W macro-floral photography + bold serif product names. Innovative temporal notes ('First / Then / Finally') with descriptor tags (Sweet|Woody|Vanilla) and intensity variants (Personal/Expressive/Bold) on a Scent Space chart. 4.9★ / 932 reviews shown prominently. Discovery kits credit toward full size.

sites.name: D.S. & Durga
sites.notes: Magazine-grade editorial: oversized type, category-labeled grid, fragrance-finder QUIZ. Best-in-class literary copy — 'Liner Notes' designer essays + Spotify playlists position scent as art object. Classic Top/Heart/Base pyramid. No reviews (authority via narrative) — its one weakness. Premium price ($225/50ml).

sites.name: Phlur
sites.notes: Emotional, memory-led copy (the 'Missing Person' phenomenon). Strong social proof and TikTok-driven demand. Aggressive email-capture popup on entry.

sites.name: Vilhelm Parfumerie
sites.notes: Cinematic dark-luxury art direction — oversized gold serif ('DEAR LORD') over moody macro. Beautiful but heavy; thinner social proof and conversion scaffolding.

sites.name: Heretic Parfum
sites.notes: Dark, provocative, masculine-leaning art direction (armored hand + amber bottle). Distinct edgy voice. Entry popup 'Step 1: Unlock 10% off'.

sites.name: Boy Smells
sites.notes: Bold, colorful 'genderful' identity with confident type system and clean grid.

sites.name: Dossier
sites.notes: Value positioning ('inspired-by' designer scents) with strong review volume and frictionless conversion. Branding intentionally clean/neutral, so lower emotional pull.

sites.name: Byredo
sites.notes: Iconic minimalist wordmark + restraint = pure luxury signal. Included as a non-Shopify design benchmark. Sparse copy, no reviews, cookie-wall friction, heavy assets.

sites.name: Goldfield & Banks
sites.notes: Polished Australian-luxe niche site; solid but less differentiated hero and thinner social proof.

weak_archetypes.name: The Discount Reseller
problems: Cluttered mega-menu + dense product grid; no visual hierarchy
No scent storytelling — SKUs treated as commodities
Banner/sale overload competes with itself; trust eroded
Notes (if any) buried in a spec table, not a pyramid
Design feels dated; price is the only differentiator

weak_archetypes.name: The Default-Theme Dropshipper
problems: Stock photography, no original art direction or identity
Fake urgency timers and 'X people viewing' widgets erode trust
Thin/auto-generated copy; no origin or maker story
No sampling, no reviews that read as real, weak returns policy
Generic type + default colors = forgettable brand

weak_archetypes.name: The Over-Designed but Broken
problems: Slow LCP from huge unoptimized video/imagery
Mystery-meat navigation; unclear what to click
Animations block reading and the CTA on scroll
Hero looks great but buries price, notes and 'add to cart'
Poor mobile rendering; layout shifts hurt confidence
